title: Native image I/O — macOS via CoreGraphics + ImageIO
goal: Implement the macOS backend for the image-I/O dispatch table defined in ESH-0006. Uses CGImageSource (read), CGImageDestination (write), and CoreGraphics bitmap contexts (resize). Native to the platform; no third-party libraries.
next_actions: lib/core/image_io_darwin.mm: implement the three function-pointer slots (read, write, resize) defined in lib/core/image_io_platform.h.
Read: CGImageSourceCreateWithURL + CGImageSourceCreateImageAtIndex + CGImageGetWidth/Height + CGContextDrawImage into a uint8_t* buffer.
Write: CGImageDestinationCreateWithURL + CGImageDestinationAddImage + CGImageDestinationFinalize. Format selected via UTI (kUTTypePNG, kUTTypeJPEG, etc.).
Resize: CGContext at the target dimensions + CGContextDrawImage(rect, src).
Wire into CMakeLists.txt: APPLE branch links CoreGraphics and ImageIO frameworks; selects this .mm file instead of stb_image.
Add tests/v1_3_edge_cases/image_io_darwin_test.esk: read a small PNG fixture, assert shape; round-trip write+re-read with checksum equality; resize 16x16 to 8x8 and verify pixel count.
acceptance: lib/core/image_io_darwin.mm provides read/write/resize matching the contract in ESH-0006.
macOS build links only CoreGraphics + ImageIO (no deps/stb).
